channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kenya’s progressive approach to nation-building and governance reform
item.description: The article 'Evolving model of national transformation' explores how Kenya’s development philosophy and governance model have gradually shifted to match new realities in social, political, and economi...

item.title: Kenyan Youths and the Rise of Fraudulent ‘Sharp Boys’ Culture
item.description: The article sheds light on a growing phenomenon among Kenyan youths known as 'sharp boys'—young men who depend on tricks and scams to make money in tough economic times. At first, the phrase suggested...

item.title: Kenya’s Empty Coffers and the Myth of Missing Corruption Money
item.description: This article was meant to be an opinion piece that reflects on Kenya’s current financial situation. From the title, it seems the writer was addressing the common phrase that there’s no money left to s...

item.title: Trump’s Potential Endorsement Could Shape California’s Republican Gubernatorial Race
item.description: The upcoming California governor’s race has become a high-stakes contest where two leading Republicans, Riverside County Sheriff Chad Bianco and conservative commentator Steve Hilton, are vying for Do...

item.title: Internal Tensions Challenge Raila Odinga’s Standing Within ODM
item.description: The Orange Democratic Movement (ODM), one of Kenya’s largest political parties, is facing internal rifts that could taint the legacy of its long-time leader, Raila Odinga. Reports suggest growing divi...

item.title: Veron Mosengo-Omba steps down as CAF Secretary General following internal challenges in African football
item.description: The Confederation of African Football (CAF) is facing another wave of leadership instability after its Secretary General, Veron Mosengo-Omba, stepped down from his position. Mosengo-Omba, who has been...

item.title: Pakistan and Saudi Arabia reaffirm unity and cooperation amid Middle East crisis
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif appreciated Saudi Arabia’s ‘remarkable restraint’ amid the ongoing Middle East crisis during a meeting with Saudi Foreign Minister Prince Faisal bin Farhan Al Saud in Isl...

item.title: Police allege PSL security protocol breach involving Shaheen Afridi and Sikandar Raza
item.description: According to the report, Lahore Deputy Inspector-General of Police (Operations) Muhammad Faisal informed the Pakistan Super League CEO about an alleged breach of security protocols involving Lahore Qa...

item.title: Human-wildlife conflict in Kilifi leaves Grade 7 pupil dead, schools in fear
item.description: A Grade 7 pupil from a school in Kilifi County lost his life after being attacked by a lion, an incident that has reignited fears about rising human-wildlife conflict along Kenya’s coastal region. Rep...

item.title: Karachi Police Grants Two-Day Extension for Installing Official Vehicle Number Plates Ahead of Crackdown
item.description: Karachi police have announced a two-day extension for vehicle owners to install government-approved number plates. The new deadline is now April 1, 2026, instead of the previous March 30 date. This de...

item.title: Court bars government from taking part in Nairobi Hospital’s management decisions
item.description: The High Court in Nairobi has ruled that the government should not interfere with how Nairobi Hospital is managed. The judgment came after a dispute involving the hospital’s board and some state offic...

item.title: PIA resumes direct flights from Islamabad to London after six-year pause
item.description: Pakistan International Airlines (PIA) has successfully resumed its direct flights to London after a break of six years. The first flight, PK-785, departed from Islamabad International Airport on Sunda...

item.title: Pakistan offers to host and facilitate possible Iran-US talks amid regional tensions
item.description: Pakistan has stepped up its diplomatic efforts to help cool tensions in the Middle East and may soon host talks between Iran and the United States. Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar...

item.title: Pakistan offers to host US-Iran talks as FM Dar holds quadrilateral meeting with Saudi, Turkish and Egyptian counterparts
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar stated on Sunday that both Iran and the United States have expressed full confidence in Pakistan to facilitate dialogue amid the ongoing Middle Eas...

item.title: Governor Nyaribo distances himself from officers over Kericho mass grave probe
item.description: Governor Amos Nyaribo of Nyamira County has sparked controversy after distancing himself from his officers following revelations linked to a mass grave in Kericho County. The grim discovery, believed ...

item.title: Court directs former Nairobi official to refund Sh13 million from cemetery land scandal
item.description: A former Nairobi City County official has been ordered by the High Court to refund more than Sh13 million that he received as an illegal kickback in a controversial cemetery land transaction. The case...

item.title: Pakistan hosts quadrilateral meeting with Saudi Arabia, Turkiye and Egypt to discuss Middle East de-escalation
item.description: Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar stated that Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Turkiye and Egypt held detailed discussions in Islamabad on ways to bring an early and permanent end to the ongoing Middle East war. The ...

item.title: Pakistan to host US-Iran talks as FM Dar highlights regional support for de-escalation
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar stated on Sunday that both Iran and the United States have shown confidence in Pakistan's ability to facilitate direct talks amid the ongoing Middl...

item.title: Veron Mossengo-Omba resigns as CAF General Secretary amid leadership challenges
item.description: The Confederation of African Football (CAF) General Secretary, Veron Mossengo-Omba, has stepped down from his position, bringing an end to a controversial period in African football administration. Hi...

item.title: Mtwara Court Sentences Woman to 30 Years for Cannabis Possession
item.description: A 60-year-old woman, Hawa Ally, has been sentenced to 30 years in prison by the Mtwara District Magistrate Court for unlawful possession of narcotic drugs. The sentence, handed down on March 27, 2026,...
